name: Flags of the World
description: All 197 national flags: every UN member and observer state, plus Kosovo and Taiwan, with notes on the lookalike pairs.

fields.extra: Mexico uses the same green-white-red, but darker shades with a coat of arms in the center.
Green, white, orange. Côte d'Ivoire uses the same colors in reverse order, orange at the hoist.
The Union Jack combines the crosses of England, Scotland, and Ireland.
Luxembourg looks nearly identical; its blue stripe is noticeably lighter.
One of only two square national flags, along with Vatican City.
White over red. Indonesia and Monaco are the other way around: red over white.
The Dannebrog, often cited as the oldest national flag still in use.
Chad's flag is nearly identical; Romania's blue is slightly lighter.
Nearly identical to Indonesia; Monaco's flag has shorter proportions.
Bangladesh also centers a single disc: red on a green field.
Red over white. Monaco is nearly identical, and Poland is the inverse.
Flown with the red stripe on top in wartime.
The black star gives Ghana's national team its nickname, the Black Stars.
Nearly identical to Romania; Chad's blue is slightly darker.
Italy uses the same three colors, but brighter and without the coat of arms.
Uruguay shares the sky blue, white, and Sun of May.
Ecuador and Venezuela share the same tricolor; Ecuador adds a coat of arms, Venezuela adds stars.
Often confused with the Texas state flag, whose blue band runs the full height.
The only national flag with none of red, white, or blue.
New Zealand's flag is similar; Australia adds the large Commonwealth Star and shows white stars.
Four red stars edged in white; no Commonwealth Star, unlike Australia.
Haiti's civil flag was once identical; Liechtenstein added the gold crown after the 1936 Olympics.
Nearly identical to the Netherlands; the blue stripe is lighter.
White, blue, red horizontal. Slovakia and Slovenia use the same stripes plus a coat of arms.
Russia's stripes with a double-cross shield toward the hoist.
Russia's stripes with the Triglav coat of arms toward the hoist.
The other square national flag, along with Switzerland.
Red with five white serrated points. Qatar is maroon with nine.
A red disc on green, like Japan's on white; the disc sits slightly toward the hoist.
The only national flag with a building: Angkor Wat.
One of the few flags showing a map of the country.
Like the Palestinian flag with a white seven-pointed star in the chevron.
Stripes like the US flag, with a crescent and star in the canton.
The only non-quadrilateral national flag: two stacked pennants.
Maroon with nine white serrated points. Bahrain is brighter red with five.

fields.back: The Democratic Republic of the Congo

fields.extra: Orange, white, green vertical. Ireland is the reverse, with green at the hoist.
Mali's colors in reverse: red at the hoist.
One star and eleven stripes, modeled on the United States flag.
Green, yellow, red vertical. Senegal adds a green star; Guinea reverses the colors.
The only national flag with a modern rifle on it.
Like Mali with a green star in the center.
Colombia's tricolor with the national coat of arms in the center.
The only national flag with different emblems on its two sides.
Sun of May and sky-blue stripes, like Argentina.
Colombia's tricolor with an arc of eight stars.
A yellow disc on blue, like Japan's design; the disc is the full moon, not the sun.
